source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
title: Ensure Audit Logging is Enabled

input_row.source: CIS AWS DATABASE SERVICES BENCHMARK V1.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure Audit Logging is Enabled
input_row.description: To manage your enterprise caching solution, it is important that you know how your clusters are performing and the resources they are consuming. It is also important that you know the events that are being generated and the costs of your deployment. Amazon CloudWatch provides metrics for monitoring your cache performance. In addition, cost allocation tags help you monitor and manage costs.
input_row.rationale: Impact: Reduce the risk of any fraud or inconsistency within the database because only authorized user has access to it.
input_row.audit: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console • Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Open the Amazon Keyspaces Console • Navigate to the service using the Find Services search bar or by directly accessing the console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/keyspaces/. 3. Select the Keyspace • Choose the Keyspace (database) for which you want to enable audit logging. • Click on the Keyspace name to access its details page. 4. Enable Amazon CloudWatch Logs • In the Keyspace details page, click on the Configuration tab. • Under the Logging section, locate the CloudWatch Logs option. • Click on Edit. • Select the Enable option to enable logging for the Keyspace. • Choose an existing CloudWatch Logs log group or create a new one to store the logs generated by the Keyspace activities. • Click Save to enable CloudWatch Logs for the Keyspace.  5. Configure CloudWatch Logs • Open the CloudWatch console by navigating to CloudWatch in the AWS Management Console. • In the left-side menu, click on Logs. • Create a new log group or select an existing log group that will store the Keyspaces logs. • Configure log retention settings based on your retention requirements. Logs can be stored for a specific number of days or indefinitely. • Define any necessary log group permissions to control access to the logs. • Optionally, set up log exports or alarms for specific log events or patterns if needed. 6. Verify the Logging Status • Wait a few minutes for the changes to propagate and the logging configuration to take effect. • Refresh the Keyspace details page to see the updated logging status. • Verify that CloudWatch Logs is enabled for the Keyspace. 7. Monitor and Analyze Logs • Navigate to the CloudWatch console and select the log group that stores the Keyspaces logs. • Monitor the logs to gain insights into the activities and operations performed on your Keyspace. • Use CloudWatch Logs features, such as log searching, filtering, and visualization, to analyze the logs and identify any security or operational issues. • Establish appropriate log monitoring and alerting mechanisms to proactively identify and respond to potential security incidents or operational anomalies.
input_row.impact: Reduce the risk of any fraud or inconsistency within the database because only authorized user has access to it.
input_row.justification: While the first AI opinion suggests automation via AWS APIs for Amazon Keyspaces, this is technically inaccurate—Amazon Keyspaces does not currently support audit logging in the manner described (i.e., with CloudWatch Logs integration for audit events). The second AI correctly points out this limitation. There are no boto3 or AWS CLI methods to programmatically verify audit logging for Amazon Keyspaces as described in the control. Therefore, this control cannot be fully automated and requires manual verification.
input_row.manual_summary: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials. 2. Navigate to the Amazon Keyspaces console: https://console.aws.amazon.com/keyspaces/. 3. Select the Keyspace (database) you wish to review. 4. Review the available configuration options and documentation to determine if any audit logging or monitoring features are present (as of this writing, Keyspaces does not natively support audit logging to CloudWatch Logs). 5. If your organization has implemented custom logging (e.g., via application-level logging or VPC flow logs), verify that these are enabled and properly configured. 6. Document your findings, noting the absence of native audit logging features and any compensating controls in place.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit detail concludes that Amazon Keyspaces currently does not support audit logging in the way described (via CloudWatch Logs), indicating that it cannot be fully automated. No available AWS APIs, boto3 methods, or AWS CLI commands can verify this specific logging feature. Thus, verification requires manual inspection within the AWS Management Console.

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/ with your AWS account credentials.
2. Navigate to the Amazon Keyspaces console by directly accessing https://console.aws.amazon.com/keyspaces/.
3. Select the Keyspace (database) you wish to review by clicking on its name to access its details.
4. Review the available configuration options and documentation to determine if any audit logging or monitoring features are present (as noted, Keyspaces does not support native audit logging to CloudWatch Logs).
5. If your organization has implemented custom logging mechanisms (e.g., via application-level logging or VPC flow logs), verify that these are enabled and appropriately configured.
6. Document your findings, noting the absence of native audit logging features within Amazon Keyspaces and any compensating controls that may be implemented. 

This thorough review and documentation process will help in assessing compliance with the control while highlighting any limitations or compensating mechanisms in place.
